title: Seeds, Replay, Settings, Save Data, and Migration · Runtime Lens: State Migration
summary: A seed is saved without algorithm version, action times drive logic, JSON fields are trusted, and corrupted data erases the previous session. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the state migration increment in versioned replay and safe-state schema. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Migrate supported versions explicitly, reject unsupported or corrupted data safely, and retain recovery evidence.
Write or replace state through a controlled adapter that preserves the last known good save on failure.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example puzzle team handles case 3832 during a museum kiosk accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate replay identity, logical actions, bounded JSON state, atomic replacement, and version migration; event-loop work must remain cancellable; then change display scale and retain focus and control access. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Migrate supported versions explicitly, reject unsupported or corrupted data safely, and retain recovery evidence. A defensible solution applies State Migration, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to versioned replay and safe-state schema.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-determinism-replay-save-2
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-determinism-replay-save-2.
# Case 7417: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def worked_maze_determinism_replay_save_2(recorded_actions: int = 36, replayed_actions: int = 36, schema_version: int = 3, invalid_fields: int = 0, replay_digest_match: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "gridworked2",
        "topology7417",
        "search2dekrow",
        "eventworked27417",
        "access7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if recorded_actions < 0 or replayed_actions < 0:
        return False, replayed_actions, "replay-count"
    if replayed_actions != recorded_actions:
        return False, replayed_actions, "action-reconciliation"
    if schema_version < 1 or invalid_fields > 0:
        return False, invalid_fields, "schema-validation"
    if not replay_digest_match:
        return False, replayed_actions, "replay-divergence"
    assert replayed_actions == recorded_actions
    return True, replayed_actions, "versioned-replay-state"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Build State Migration evidence · Theory State Migration
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: maze-state-migration-1fiwbv" that uses case 6222 to validate replay identity, logical actions, bounded JSON state, atomic replacement, and version migration. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-state-migration-1fiwbv
# Competency: maze-state-migration.
# Case 6222: a low-vision player session.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it.
def evidence_maze_state_migration_1fiwbv(recorded_actions: int = 36, replayed_actions: int = 36, schema_version: int = 3, invalid_fields: int = 0, replay_digest_match: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1fiwbv = "1fiwbv-vbwif1-6222"
    assert evidence_variant_1fiwbv
    evidence_axes_1fiwbv = (
        "grid1fiwbv",
        "topology6222",
        "searchvbwif1",
        "event1fiwbv6222",
        "access62221fiwbv",
        "recoveryvbwif16222",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1fiwbv)) == 6
    if recorded_actions < 0 or replayed_actions < 0:
        return False, replayed_actions, "replay-count"
    if replayed_actions != recorded_actions:
        return False, replayed_actions, "action-reconciliation"
    if schema_version < 1 or invalid_fields > 0:
        return False, invalid_fields, "schema-validation"
    if not replay_digest_match:
        return False, replayed_actions, "replay-divergence"
    assert replayed_actions == recorded_actions
    return True, replayed_actions, "versioned-replay-state"

Build deterministic Python evidence to validate replay identity, logical actions, bounded JSON state, atomic replacement, and version migration; resource use must remain below an explicit budget; then inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Migrate supported versions explicitly, reject unsupported or corrupted data safely, and retain recovery evidence.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the State Migration model · Theory State Migration, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Adding defaults silently makes every old save compatible.
